question: What should I know before editing?
title: Developer notes
summary: Warnings, lessons, and failure paths that survived real incidents—not generic best practices.

items.title: Migration exists and is documented
items.intro: AI will write forms before columns exist unless you stop it.
checks: Guide chapter updated if behavior changed

steps.title: File exists?
steps.text: Check data/ideas.sqlite on the server. Deploy excludes *.sqlite by design.

steps.title: PHP can read it?
steps.text: File permissions and path in includes/db.php must match hosting.

steps.title: Query returns rows?
steps.text: Schema drift: column added locally but migration not applied in production.

problems.title: Saved fields disappear
problems.note: All five must agree. Fix the mismatch, then update lessons-learned if a new pattern appeared.

steps.title: Compare form names to SQL
steps.text: Diff POST keys against INSERT columns.

steps.title: Compare write to read
steps.text: SELECT must project every field the template displays.

entries.question: What breaks if I add authentication?

points.title: Scope explosion
points.text: Sessions, passwords, recovery, threat model—none of it exists yet.

points.title: Documented trap
points.text: Halfway auth is worse than none. See warnings/no-halfway-auth.md.

points.title: Required first step
points.text: New decision file + guide update before any code.

entries.question: What breaks if I change list ordering?

points.title: User expectation
points.text: Manual priority is the product behavior operators rely on.

points.title: Query coupling
points.text: List handler and sort_order column must change together.

entries.question: What breaks if I switch databases?

points.title: Deploy story
points.text: Backup, connection config, migration path—all change.

points.title: Decision required
points.text: Read considered-postgresql.md; do not switch in a drive-by refactor.

cards.title: Schema before code
cards.teaser: Migration and forms in the same change set.
points: Now standard in every schema task
